title: boltonia apalachicolensis
intro: Boltonia apalachicolensis, also known as Apalachicola false aster, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Boltonia apalachicolensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for skin conditions and injuries. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and has also been used in folk medicine as a poultice or compress to treat various health conditions.
